Bay Delta Trust

Activities
33314415-10e1-810b-bbef-d7c19ffb2c24, 33314415-10e1-81b7-a893-ffc7750e6e9b, 33314415-10e1-8180-972a-ee6499aaa3da
type
organization
location_lat
37.8
background_section
## Background In the San Francisco Bay–Sacramento–San Joaquin River Delta bioregion, Coast Miwok and Ohlone peoples and their ancestors sustained relations with the land and water over countless generations—interwoven, like coast redwoods that support one another though their roots are shallow, so the grove stands. More recently, Peter Berg—an early voice for bioregionalism—made San Francisco a base. In the mid-1980s he convened Bay Area groups to draft the [Green City Program for urban sustainability](https://youtu.be/Vgt9g24gi7Q), published in 1989, and helped organize annual Shasta Bioregion Gatherings across Northern California. Today’s initiatives draw on both Indigenous continuity and that later bioregional lineage. ### Origins and Network Lawrence Grodeska founded the Bay-Delta Trust, a weaving and convening initiative seeking to improve network coordination and increase regenerative investments in their bioregion. The organization, fiscally sponsored by the Buckminster Fuller Institute, explores new place-based models of pluralistic governance and collaboration.

Bay Delta Community Learning Ecosystem|Bay Delta Asset Mapping|Bay Delta Governance Innovation
description
The Bay-Delta Trust, fiscally sponsored by the Buckminster Fuller Institute, operates as a weaving and convening initiative focused on improving network coordination and increasing regenerative investments in the bioregion. Their approach builds on a rich history of bioregional work in the area, including Peter Berg's influential contributions through the Planet Drum Foundation in the 1970s, which helped establish early municipal environmental programs.
id
bay-delta-trust
vision_legacy_section
## Vision and Legacy The Trust describes itself as still in a discovery phase: listening, interviewing, and mapping partners with humility before large commitments. The aim is a 10,000-day horizon toward a 100-year vision for the Bay–Delta—a container where anyone can contribute how they imagine quality of life and a livable watershed.
website
https://baydelta.notion.site/The-Bay-Delta-Trust-c59dc12d6ce14f858e6bfdfbe4fb7d54
bioregional_context
The San Francisco Bay-Delta bioregion encompasses the largest tidal estuary on the west coast of the Americas, centered around the San Francisco Bay and Sacramento-San Joaquin River Delta. The region is defined by its watershed drainage basin, which extends well beyond the immediate bay area to include multiple counties in California. The Mediterranean climate shapes key regional concerns around water, food, and fire management.
category
Bioregional Organizer
